name: Yemen

colors.symbolism: the unity of the Yemeni nation and the bloodshed of those who fought for freedom
the dark days of the past, including foreign occupation and pre-revolutionary regimes

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — red on top, white in the middle, black at the bottom — a plain Arab Liberation tricolour with no charge.
history: Adopted on 22 May 1990 with the unification of the Yemen Arab Republic (North Yemen) and the People's Democratic Republic of Yemen (South Yemen). The red-white-black tricolour is the Arab Liberation Flag, originally devised for the Egyptian revolution of 1952 and used by other Arab nationalist republics. Both predecessor states had used charged versions of the same tricolour: North Yemen had a green star on the white stripe, while South Yemen had a light-blue triangle at the hoist with a red star.

features.symbolism: the colours of the Arab Liberation Flag — red for the bloody struggle for independence and unity, white for a bright future, black for the dark days of the past
